title: `links init` — scaffold a new link page project
description: Implement the `links init` command. When run in an empty or new directory, it creates a links.yaml with sensible defaults and prints a helpful getting-started message. Should be safe to run — never overwrite an existing links.yaml without confirmation.

features: Implement src/commands/init.js — async function that creates links.yaml in cwd
If links.yaml already exists in cwd: print warning 'links.yaml already exists. Use --force to overwrite.' and exit 1 unless --force flag is passed

Generated links.yaml content: name field (prompted from user or uses system username as default), bio field (empty placeholder with comment), avatar field (commented out example), theme field (minimal-dark), domain field (commented out example), links array with two example entries (one placeholder social, one placeholder website)
Use YAML comments in the generated file to guide the user (e.g., '# Your page URL once deployed')
After writing, print success message with next steps: 'Created links.yaml', 'Next: links add <label> <url>', 'Deploy: links deploy --self --out ./dist'
Accept optional positional argument: `links init [directory]` — if provided, init in that directory (create if not exists)

notes:
[GIA REJECTED] Hook(s) failed: 001-adr-compliance.py. Alignment score 0.8333 below threshold 1.0000
No interactive prompts for MVP — just create the file with defaults. Prompts can be a future enhancement. YAML comment syntax via js-yaml dump options or manual string template.
